data.title: Xiaomi's mobile AI Agent "Xiaomi miclaw" begins limited closed beta testing.
data.description: Xiaomi miclaw, an AI interaction testing product built on Xiaomi's MiMo large-scale model, began its limited closed beta test today, by invitation only, with no public recruitment. Running as a system application, Xiaomi miclaw encapsulates over 50 system-level tools and ecosystem services, employing an inference-execution loop engine where the model autonomously determines the tool invocation order and parameters. This product focuses on verifying the execution capabilities of the large-scale model within Xiaomi's "Human, Vehicle, and Home Ecosystem," featuring a four-layer architecture: system-level capabilities, personal contextual understanding, ecosystem interconnection, and self-evolution. Regarding ecosystem interconnection, Xiaomi miclaw implements a complete Mi Home protocol client, allowing control of IoT devices connected to Mi Home with user authorization, and supports the MCP protocol and third-party application access SDKs. In terms of self-evolution, the product possesses four meta-capabilities: file-level memory, sub-agent creation, MCP service configuration, and sandbox script execution, enabling it to autonomously build a memory system and expand its tool scope. Xiaomi notes that the product is still under optimization in terms of stability, power consumption, and success rate in complex scenarios; users are advised to back up their data beforehand and test in a controlled environment.

data.title: BitMEX: OPNUSDT perpetual contracts launched, with leverage up to 50x.
data.description: According to official sources, OPNUSDT perpetual contracts have been launched, with leverage up to 50x available.

data.title: AI and Crypto: The Emerging Synergy in Web3
data.description:
Crypto KOL Bitwu.ETH posted on X that the intersection of AI and cryptocurrency represents a significant growth opportunity in the Web3 space this year. According to Bitwu.ETH, the real need is not for crypto to adopt AI, but for AI to integrate crypto, particularly in establishing a payment system between machines.

The first challenge highlighted is the payment issue. AI agents will require extensive interaction, and the market for AI assistant transactions could reach trillions in the coming years. Traditional banking systems are inadequate for automatic transactions, API calls, and purchasing computing power or data. Currently, only crypto can offer permissionless and programmable payment solutions.

Secondly, the global settlement problem is addressed. AI operates on a global network, necessitating an international financial system for transactions between AI agents located in different countries, such as Germany and South Korea. Blockchain technology naturally serves as a cross-border settlement layer.

The third issue is the ownership of digital scarce assets in the AI era, including data, models, and computing power. Tokens and blockchain present a potential solution for proving ownership.

Bitwu.ETH notes that while 90% of the current AI and crypto narrative is still speculative, the genuine demand and projects will emerge as AI agents become more prevalent. Identifying valuable opportunities in this space requires active engagement with AI agents and exploring various interactions with AI. The goal is not merely to profit from AI but to enhance personal efficiency and discover lucrative opportunities within the market without straying too far from it.

data.title: 2001 Golden Globe winner Michael Owen will serve as a ForeGate global ambassador.
data.description: ForeGate announced on its X platform that 2001 Golden Globe winner Michael Owen will serve as its global ambassador.

data.title: Malaysia Delays 2026 Growth Forecast Amid Middle East Uncertainty
data.description: Malaysia's second finance minister announced that the government will postpone raising its 2026 growth forecast due to uncertainties stemming from the Middle East conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on Malaysia's economic outlook. The decision reflects concerns over potential disruptions in global markets and trade, which could affect Malaysia's economic performance in the coming years. The minister emphasized the need for cautious economic planning in light of these uncertainties.

data.title: DeFiLlama: The top two CEXs with the largest net inflows of funds in the past 24 hours are Bitget and Bybit.
data.description: According to DeFiLlama data, the top two trading platforms with the largest net inflows of funds in the past 24 hours were Bitget ($126 million) and Bybit ($108 million).

data.title: SoftBank Seeks Record $40 Billion Loan for OpenAI Stake Acquisition
data.description: SoftBank Group is reportedly pursuing a record-breaking loan of up to $40 billion to acquire a stake in OpenAI. According to Jin10, the Japanese conglomerate aims to expand its investment portfolio by securing a significant position in the AI research organization. This move reflects SoftBank's strategic interest in advancing its presence in the artificial intelligence sector, which is increasingly becoming a focal point for global tech investments. The acquisition, if successful, would mark one of the largest financial undertakings by SoftBank, highlighting its commitment to leveraging AI technologies for future growth. The details of the loan and acquisition process are yet to be finalized, and further developments are anticipated as negotiations progress.

data.title: OpenAI to Lend Funds from Nvidia and Amazon to SoftBank
data.description: OpenAI plans to lend funds obtained through cyclical financing from Nvidia and Amazon to SoftBank. According to Jin10, this strategic move aims to enhance collaboration and investment opportunities between these major tech companies. The financial arrangement underscores the growing interconnectedness within the tech industry, as companies seek to leverage each other's strengths and resources for mutual benefit. This development highlights the dynamic nature of partnerships and investments in the tech sector, reflecting a trend towards increased cooperation among leading firms.

data.title: China's Economic Shift Towards Consumer Spending to Be Gradual, Says Central Bank Adviser
data.description: China's transition towards a consumer-driven economy is expected to be a prolonged process, according to a central bank adviser. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the nation in shifting its economic focus from investment and exports to consumer spending. The adviser emphasized that while the government is committed to this transformation, it will require significant time and effort to achieve substantial results. The shift is part of China's broader strategy to ensure sustainable economic growth and reduce reliance on external factors. The adviser noted that structural reforms and policy adjustments are necessary to facilitate this transition, which aims to enhance domestic consumption as a key driver of economic activity. The central bank's role in supporting this shift includes implementing measures to boost consumer confidence and spending power. However, the adviser cautioned that the process would not be immediate, as it involves overcoming various economic and social challenges. The gradual nature of this transition underscores the complexity of reorienting the world's second-largest economy towards a more balanced and resilient growth model.

data.title: Vancouver Reconsiders Bitcoin Initiative Amid Legal Constraints
data.description: Vancouver city officials have advised the council to abandon its initiative to become a "Bitcoin-friendly city." According to NS3.AI, a city report revealed that Bitcoin is not a permissible investment under the Vancouver Charter. The initiative had included plans to explore accepting taxes and fees in cryptocurrency. The report suggests closing the agenda item, as the provincial government has stated that local governments are prohibited from holding cryptocurrency due to financial risk legislation.

data.title: Asian High-Yield Dollar Bonds Face Pressure Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Asian high-yield dollar bonds are experiencing significant pressure compared to their global counterparts due to the escalating conflict involving Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the region's heavy dependence on imported energy, which contrasts sharply with the situation in the United States. The ongoing geopolitical tensions have intensified concerns over energy security in Asia, affecting investor sentiment and bond performance. As the situation develops, market participants are closely monitoring the impact on Asian economies and their financial markets.

data.title: China's Solar Energy Goals Reflect Industry Challenges
data.description: China has opted not to establish ambitious targets for solar energy in its latest five-year plan, signaling the industry's growing challenges following years of rapid expansion. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that the decision underscores the difficulties faced by the sector as it grapples with issues such as market saturation and technological hurdles. The move marks a shift from previous plans that aggressively promoted solar energy development, reflecting a more cautious approach amid evolving industry dynamics.

data.title: Xi-Trump Meeting Faces Tension Amid Iran Attack
data.description: The longstanding friendship between Beijing and Tehran is under strain following a recent attack on Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this development could lead to an awkward meeting between Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump later this month. The geopolitical dynamics between the two nations are expected to be a focal point during their discussions.

data.title: Iranian Official Reports No Military Casualties in Recent Attack
data.description: An Iranian deputy governor for political and security affairs in Qom Province has stated that a recent attack did not result in any military casualties. According to Jin10, the official emphasized that the incident did not impact military personnel, although further details about the attack were not disclosed. The statement comes amid heightened tensions in the region, with various geopolitical factors influencing security dynamics.

data.title: The City of Vancouver has recommended shutting down the Bitcoin reserve proposal after legal review.
data.description: Vancouver city staff have recommended closing a council motion exploring the city's potential to become a "Bitcoin-friendly city," arguing that a review determined the Vancouver Charter does not permit holding Bitcoin as a municipal reserve asset. In a report submitted to the city council, staff stated that it had been "finalized" that Bitcoin is not a "permitted investment asset" and recommended closing the motion within a framework of realigning resources and priorities. The proposal stemmed from a motion pushed by Mayor Ken Sim over a year ago, which asked staff to study the city's ability to accept cryptocurrency tax payments and the possibility of converting some of its fiscal reserves into Bitcoin. The British Columbia Ministry of Municipal Affairs had previously stated that provincial regulations prohibit municipalities from holding fiscal reserves in cryptocurrency, with legislation aimed at ensuring local government funds are not subjected to excessive risk. Pete Fry, the sole opponent of the motion in council, told local media that he had previously believed the proposal had been shelved. Kevin Lee, chief commercial officer of cryptocurrency exchange Gate, told Decrypt that the legal and financial obstacles were understood from the outset, and closing the process was not surprising. According to Zeus Research analyst Dominick John, municipal treasuries are structured with capital preservation in mind, and similar cities will remain in the research phase until legislation, accounting practices, and trusteeship frameworks are perfected.

data.title: Indonesia's Foreign Exchange Reserves Decline to $151.9 Billion by End of February
data.description: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves have decreased to $151.9 billion by the end of February. According to Jin10, this marks a decline in the country's reserves, reflecting changes in the economic landscape. The reduction in reserves could have implications for Indonesia's financial stability and its ability to manage external economic pressures. The central bank may need to consider strategies to bolster reserves and ensure economic resilience in the face of global uncertainties.

data.title: Denso Shares Decline Following Reported Rohm Takeover Bid
data.description: Denso shares experienced a decline in Tokyo trading on Friday after reports emerged about the company's takeover approach to Rohm. Bloomberg posted on X that the acquisition bid values the chipmaker at approximately $8.3 billion. The news has sparked interest in the market, reflecting the potential impact of such a significant acquisition in the semiconductor industry. The proposed deal highlights Denso's strategic move to strengthen its position in the chip manufacturing sector.

data.title: Hong Kong's Economic Growth Forecast Revised Upward for 2023
data.description: Hong Kong's economic growth for this year is anticipated to surpass earlier predictions, driven by enhanced financial activity and government expansionary policies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the positive outlook following the government's budget announcement on February 25. The revised forecast reflects optimism in the region's economic prospects, bolstered by strategic fiscal measures aimed at stimulating growth. Analysts suggest that these factors could contribute to a more robust economic performance for Hong Kong in 2023.

data.title: Russia Proposes Crypto Trading Licenses for Banks with 1% Exposure Cap
data.description: The Central Bank of Russia has introduced a legislative proposal aimed at allowing banks to acquire crypto trading licenses. According to NS3.AI, this initiative includes a stipulation that limits banks' exposure to cryptocurrencies to 1% of their own capital. The proposal outlines a streamlined notification process for banks and brokerage firms to begin offering crypto brokerage services, leveraging their current licenses. The deadline for the adoption of this bill is set for July 1.

data.title: 21Shares announced that the 21Shares Polkadot ETF will soon be launched on Nasdaq.
data.description: According to official news, 21Shares US announced that its Polkadot spot ETF—the 21Shares Polkadot ETF—will soon be launched on Nasdaq.
